descriptions: The Ciaculli and Croceverde Giardini factions of the Greco clan engaged in a violent feud from 1939 to 1947.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1939 a bloody vendetta between both clans started during a brawl about a question of honour among youngsters of the two clans.

descriptions: Muto and Pranno were both targeted in the same operation, indicating they are distinct groups.
edges.evidence_quote: 10 luglio 2002 - operazione "Star Price" contro i Pranno e i Muto.

descriptions: Morabito maintains strong ties with Mollica.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morabitos also maintain strong ties with the Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco, and Scriva 'ndrine.

descriptions: Morabito has ties with Mollica.
edges.evidence_quote: Ma possono contare su legami con i Maisano, Pansera, Versace, Zappia, Mollica, Criaco e Scriva.

descriptions: The Cleveland Syndicate included Charles Polizzi, who was connected to the Mayfield Road Mob, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Charles Polizzi the adopted brother of Alfred Polizzi, head of the Italian Mayfield Road Mob.

descriptions: The Cleveland Syndicate and Mayfield Road Mob were connected through family ties and likely cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Charles Polizzi was born Leo Berkowitz ... his adoptive brother, Alfred Polizzi , was the head of the Italian Mayfield Road Mob

descriptions: The Pillow Gang and Russo Gang battled the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: The two gangs battled with the Green Ones until 1928, when the Russo gang lost too many members and blamed the Pillow gang for betraying them.

descriptions: After the alliance broke, the Pillow Gang fought the Russo Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1931, Fresina was murdered and members of the Pillow gang continued fighting with the Russo gang and the Green Ones.

descriptions: The Pillow Gang allied with the Russo Gang to battle the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: Fresina formed an alliance with splinter group of the Green Ones called the Russo gang led by Tony Russo.

descriptions: Black and Tans retaliated against IRA activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of both units, however, were despised by the Irish public, against whom the 'Tans' and 'Auxies' routinely retaliated against for IRA raids and assassinations.

descriptions: PKK and Dev-Sol were both colluded with by Ergenekon, but no direct relationship between them is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Former police special forces member Ayhan Çarkın said that the state, using the clandestine Ergenekon network, colluded with militant groups such as the PKK, Dev-Sol and Turkish Hezbollah, with the goal of profiting from the war.

descriptions: Tavoletta-Ucciero and Schiavone-Bidognetti fought an internal faida from 2003 to 2007.
edges.evidence_quote: terza faida interna ai Casalesi : combattuta dal 2003 al 2007 tra le famiglie Tavoletta-Ucciero e Schiavone-Bidognetti

descriptions: Inagawa-kai is part of the Kanto-Hatsukakai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: a federation of Tokyo-based yakuza groups including the Inagawa-kai and the Sumiyoshi-kai

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai is part of the Kanto-Hatsukakai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: a federation of Tokyo-based yakuza groups including the Inagawa-kai and the Sumiyoshi-kai

descriptions: Kantō Hatsukakai was a federation opposed to the Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang had long been a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai, a federation of Tokyo yakuza groups opposed to the powerful, Kansai -based Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Kokusui-kai was a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang had long been a member of the Kantō Hatsukakai, a federation of Tokyo yakuza groups opposed to the powerful, Kansai -based Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai is a member of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Toa-kai is a member of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi's expansion into Tokyo would create conflict with Kanto-Hatsukakai.
edges.evidence_quote: this movement would inevitably create conflict between the Yamaguchi-gumi and the Kanto-Hatsukakai

Kurdish mafia.description: Organised crime groups run by ethnic Kurds, operating mainly in Western Asia, Europe, North America, and Afghanistan. Their primary activities include drug and arms trafficking, contract killing, and migrant smuggling. Some groups have been accused of funneling profits to the PKK. The mafia has historical roots in banditry during and after World War I.

descriptions: Infobox lists Kurdish mafia as an ally of Armenian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Italian-American Mafia , Mexican Mafia , Russian mafia , Lebanese mafia , Italian mafia , Greek mafia , Kurdish mafia , Calabrian mafia

descriptions: The Baybaşin family is allied with the Kurdish mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kurdistan Workers' Party , Kurdish mafia

descriptions: The Kurdish mafia is listed as an ally of the Greek mafia in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Albanian mafia , American Mafia , Armenian mafia , Georgian mafia , Kurdish mafia , Baybaşin family , Serbian mafia , Sacra Corona Unita , Philadelphia Greek Mob , Velentzas crime family

descriptions: Kurdish mafia overtook Albanian mafia in people smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: The Albanian mafia was previously dominant in the people smuggling business, although the Kurdish mafia later took over the business

descriptions: Armenian mafia listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Greek mafia, Armenian mafia, Russian mafia, Iranian mafia, Taliban (formerly)

descriptions: Greek mafia listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Greek mafia, Armenian mafia, Russian mafia, Iranian mafia, Taliban (formerly)

descriptions: Kurdish mafia cooperated with Iranian criminals (Iranian mafia) in migrant smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: they cooperated with Iranian criminals to facilitate the migrant smuggling.

descriptions: Kurdish mafia allegedly funnels profits to PKK.
edges.evidence_quote: Süleyman Soylu accused them of making over US$1.5 billion per year for the PKK.

descriptions: Russian mafia listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Greek mafia, Armenian mafia, Russian mafia, Iranian mafia, Taliban (formerly)

descriptions: Taliban supplied opiates to Kurdish mafia in exchange for tax.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taliban supplied the Kurdish mafia with opiates in exchange for a tax, and the guarantee that the opiates would not be sold to Kurdish civilians, but to Europeans or Russians only.

descriptions: Turkish mafia listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Turkish mafia

descriptions: Kurdish mafia is listed as a rival of Turkish mafia in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Armenian mafia , Greek mafia , Kurdish mafia

Banda della Magliana.description: The Banda della Magliana is a mafia-type criminal organization based in Rome, Italy, active from the late 1970s until 1999. It unified local criminal groups under a single structure, engaging in kidnappings, gambling, drug trafficking, and robberies. The group had ties to other Italian mafias, Masonic lodges, far-right terrorism, and intelligence services. It was involved in several high-profile Italian crimes and mysteries.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta has relationships with all Italian mafias, including Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto e ha rapporti con tutte le mafie italiane : Cosa nostra , Camorra , Banda della Magliana , Sacra Corona Unita , Basilischi .

descriptions: Facchineri-Varone had contacts with the Banda della Magliana through its treasurer.
edges.evidence_quote: esponenti dei Facchineri-Varone a Roma hanno avuto contatti con Enrico Nicoletti, tesoriere della Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Sebastiano Romeo was involved with Banda della Magliana in the search for Aldo Moro.
edges.evidence_quote: Sebastiano Romeo , secondo Francesco Fonti viene coinvolto insieme alla Banda della Magliana nella ricerca di Aldo Moro .

descriptions: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian government claimed that the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with and tied to other criminal organizations, such as Cosa Nostra, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was tied to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was tied to other Italian criminal organizations such as the Cosa Nostra, Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana had links with 'Ndrangheta members.
edges.evidence_quote: Lucioli iniziò il suo racconto riempiendo i verbali e confessando omicidi, rapine, traffici di stupefacenti e di armi, oltre che i legami della banda con politici, cardinali, massoni, mafiosi, camorristi, ndranghetisti, servizi segreti deviati ed eversione nera.

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with 'Ndrangheta for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

descriptions: Banda della Magliana had close ties with the three southern mafias, including 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosiddetta Banda della Magliana, che ebbe stretti legami con le tre mafie meridionali

descriptions: The trial touched on links between Banda della Magliana and 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Il dibattimento finirà inevitabilmente per toccare anche i legami del gruppo con le altre organizzazioni mafiose (cosa nostra, camorra e 'ndrangheta)

descriptions: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian government claimed that the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with and tied to other criminal organizations, such as Cosa Nostra, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was tied to Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was tied to other Italian criminal organizations such as the Cosa Nostra, Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana extended its network of contacts to the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: col tempo la banda estese la propria rete di contatti alle principali organizzazioni criminali italiane, da Cosa nostra alla camorra

descriptions: Nicolino Selis, a member of the Banda, had support from the Camorra of Raffaele Cutolo.
edges.evidence_quote: forte dell'appoggio dei camorristi di Raffaele Cutolo

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana had contacts with the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: Sicilia è anche il primo a parlare dei contatti della Banda con la camorra di Lorenzo Nuvoletta e Michele Zaza

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with Camorra for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

descriptions: Banda della Magliana had close ties with the three southern mafias, including Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosiddetta Banda della Magliana, che ebbe stretti legami con le tre mafie meridionali

descriptions: Camorra boss Cutolo relayed information about the Banda della Magliana's involvement in a murder, indicating indirect contact.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Cutolo, boss della camorra, durante il processo raccontò che, secondo quanto gli avevano raccontato il suo capozona romano Nicolino Selis e Franco Giuseppucci, la mafia non c'entrava niente ma era un fatto della Banda della Magliana

descriptions: The trial touched on links between Banda della Magliana and Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: Il dibattimento finirà inevitabilmente per toccare anche i legami del gruppo con le altre organizzazioni mafiose (cosa nostra, camorra e 'ndrangheta)

descriptions: Banda della Magliana used Casamonica clan members as criminal labor in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni ottanta, per esempio, la Banda della Magliana ha spesso utilizzato componenti del clan come manovalanza criminale

descriptions: Riccardo Sibio of Clan dei Fasciani often served Banda della Magliana members.
edges.evidence_quote: Riccardo Sibio, appartenente al clan dei Fasciani e spesso al servizio anche dei componenti della Banda

descriptions: Danilo Abbruciati entered the gambling dens controlled by the Clan dei marsigliesi, indicating ties.
edges.evidence_quote: per poi entrare nel giro delle bische clandestine controllate dal Clan dei marsigliesi di Albert Bergamelli

descriptions: The arrival of the Clan dei marsigliesi changed the power dynamics in Rome's underworld, preceding the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: con l'avvento del clan dei marsigliesi ... si determinò un deciso cambiamento dei rapporti di forze all'interno della piccola e frammentata malavita capitolina

descriptions: Clan Spada contacted Franco Colò, a former Banda della Magliana member, to expand videopoker operations.
edges.evidence_quote: il gruppo degli "zingari" per allargare i propri traffici attraverso i videopoker si fossero messi in contatto con Franco Colò detto "il ciccione", storico braccio destro di Renatino De Pedis ai tempi della Banda

descriptions: Banda della Magliana agreed to finance the neofascist group in exchange for favorable psychiatric reports.
edges.evidence_quote: Nonostante il rifiuto ad operare come braccio armato del gruppo politico di Semerari, da quegli incontri uscì un accordo di massima tra il professore e la banda che prevedeva finanziamenti per il gruppo neofascista in cambio di perizie medico psichiatriche compiacenti

descriptions: Mafia Capitale has its roots in Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 2 dicembre 2014 l'ex NAR Massimo Carminati, vicino alla Banda già dalla fine degli anni ‘70, viene arrestato assieme ad alcuni complici nell'ambito dell'operazione Mafia Capitale ; chiamata come Cupola Romana o ancora Clan Carminati , questa organizzazione operava a Roma già a partire dal 2000 circa, affondando le sue radici nelle rapine dei NAR degli anni ottanta e successivamente nella stessa banda della Magliana.

descriptions: The infobox lists NAR as an ally of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: NAR, Sicilian Mafia, Camorra

descriptions: Banda della Magliana laundered money from NAR robberies and NAR members collected debts for the Banda.
edges.evidence_quote: Carminati iniziò ad affidare i proventi delle rapine di autofinanziamento effettuate con i NAR, in modo da poterli riciclare in altre attività illecite quali l'usura o lo spaccio di droga; viceversa recuperava per Giuseppucci soldi che il boss prestava a strozzo intascandosi una 'stecca' del 10-15%.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana commissioned NAR members to commit murders on their behalf.
edges.evidence_quote: la Banda, di tanto in tanto commissionava ai giovani fascisti anche di eliminare alcune persone poco gradite, come nel caso del tabaccaio romano Teodoro Pugliese, ucciso nell'aprile del 1980 da Carminati

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana and the NAR had converging interests and collaborated in the Bologna massacre cover-up.
edges.evidence_quote: La probabile convergenza d'interessi tra gli uomini della Magliana, gli ambienti dell'eversione nera e alcuni settori deviati dei servizi e della politica, trova perfetta esemplificazione nel tentativo di depistaggio legato alla strage alla stazione ferroviaria di Bologna del 2 agosto 1980, per la quale vennero riconosciuti esecutori materiali (tra gli altri) alcuni militanti dei Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari, di Valerio Fioravanti.

descriptions: Andrea Buonpadre acted as a liaison between Banda della Magliana and far-right terrorism, including NAR.
edges.evidence_quote: fa da tramite tra la banda, Cosa Nostra e il Terrorismo nero

descriptions: Nicolino Selis, a member of the Banda della Magliana, had a close friendship with Raffaele Cutolo, founder of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: una stretta amicizia con il boss campano Raffaele Cutolo, fondatore della Nuova Camorra Organizzata

descriptions: Banda della Magliana and Nuova Camorra Organizzata cooperated: Cutolo gave approval for a murder and established a drug supply channel.
edges.evidence_quote: La decisione di uccidere Nicolini venne presa dalla banda anche in virtù del beneplacito, ottenuto dal capo della Nuova Camorra Organizzata , Raffaele Cutolo , il quale, appena evaso dall'ospedale psichiatrico di Aversa, nel febbraio del 1978 organizzò un incontro con Nicolino Selis allo scopo di trovare, tra i rispettivi gruppi, una strategia compatibile con gli obiettivi di entrambi, nominando così “il Sardo” suo luogotenente nella piazza romana.

descriptions: Raffaele Cutolo of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata contacted the Banda della Magliana to locate Aldo Moro's prison.
edges.evidence_quote: Cutolo ci ha mandato un personaggio politico a parlare per vedere se sapevamo dov'era il covo di Moro

descriptions: Claudio Sicilia served as a link between Banda della Magliana and Nuova Camorra Organizzata.
edges.evidence_quote: Claudio Sicilia ... diviene ben presto l'anello di congiunzione della banda con la Nuova Camorra Organizzata di Raffaele Cutolo.

descriptions: Aldo Semerari made deals with both NCO and NF, but the text does not assert a direct relationship between Banda della Magliana and NCO.
edges.evidence_quote: He had made the same deal with Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO), as well as with the rival organization of the Cutolo, the Nuova Famiglia (NF) headed by Carmine Alfieri.

descriptions: Aldo Semerari made deals with both NCO and NF, but the text does not assert a direct relationship between Banda della Magliana and NF.
edges.evidence_quote: He had made the same deal with Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata (NCO), as well as with the rival organization of the Cutolo, the Nuova Famiglia (NF) headed by Carmine Alfieri.

descriptions: The infobox lists Proietti clan as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Proietti clan

descriptions: Clan Proietti killed a Banda della Magliana member, sparking a violent war between the two groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Il primo componente della Banda a cadere fu Franco Giuseppucci , ucciso a Piazza San Cosimato nel cuore del quartiere di Trastevere, il 13 settembre 1980 , in un agguato orchestrato da parte di esponenti del clan rivale della famiglia Proietti , detti i Pesciaroli

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was at war with the Proietti clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la guerra al clan rivale dei Proietti

descriptions: Left-wing journalists claimed the gang had links to P2.
edges.evidence_quote: Some left-wing journalists have also claimed the gang had links to neofascist militant and terrorist groups, such as the Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari (NAR), responsible for the 1980 Bologna massacre; the Italian secret services (SISMI), and political figures such as Licio Gelli, grand-master of the Freemasonic lodge Propaganda Due (P2).

descriptions: Banda della Magliana controlled the area where the Brigate Rosse held Aldo Moro prisoner.
edges.evidence_quote: il 16 marzo del 1978, quando il Presidente della DC Aldo Moro venne rapito da un commando di brigatisti, gran parte della città di Roma era controllata dagli uomini della Banda della Magliana e la stessa ubicazione della prigione del popolo ... era posto tra via Portuense e via della Magliana

descriptions: The Italian government claimed the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian government claimed that the Banda della Magliana was closely allied with and tied to other criminal organizations, such as Cosa Nostra, Camorra, and 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was tied to Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was tied to other Italian criminal organizations such as the Cosa Nostra, Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana extended its network of contacts to Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: col tempo la banda estese la propria rete di contatti alle principali organizzazioni criminali italiane, da Cosa nostra alla camorra

descriptions: The Testaccini faction of the Banda was increasingly involved with Cosa Nostra boss Giuseppe Calò.
edges.evidence_quote: sempre più compromessi con mafiosi, quali Giuseppe Calò

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was involved in the Rapido 904 massacre, which was organized by Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Sicilia testimonierà poi nel processo riguardante la strage del Rapido 904 del 1984 ... secondo lui la strage di Natale era stata voluta e organizzata da Cosa Nostra.

descriptions: Andrea Buonpadre acted as a liaison between Banda della Magliana and Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: fa da tramite tra la banda, Cosa Nostra e il Terrorismo nero

descriptions: Banda della Magliana had close ties with the three southern mafias, including Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosiddetta Banda della Magliana, che ebbe stretti legami con le tre mafie meridionali

descriptions: The trial touched on links between Banda della Magliana and Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: Il dibattimento finirà inevitabilmente per toccare anche i legami del gruppo con le altre organizzazioni mafiose (cosa nostra, camorra e 'ndrangheta)

descriptions: Marcello Colafigli, a Banda della Magliana member, maintained contacts with Mafia foggiana for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenendo rapporti con esponenti della 'ndrangheta , della camorra , della mafia foggiana e con albanesi inseriti in un cartello narcos sudamericano

descriptions: Casamonica clan allied with Banda della Magliana from 1970s to 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The rise to the conquest of the illicit traffic of Rome, occurred, starting from 1970s, with the criminal alliance of the Banda della Magliana, and lasted until the 1990s.

descriptions: The Casamonica clan had criminal collusion with the Banda della Magliana, which provided contacts.
edges.evidence_quote: Its criminal rise took place in the early 1970s, with criminal collusion with the Banda della Magliana, which provided it with initial contacts with Camorra, 'Ndrangheta and Mafia.

descriptions: A fire group killed a former member of the Banda della Magliana, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 18 ottobre 2002 un gruppo di fuoco uccide Paolo Frau , ex uomo della Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Casamonica collaborated with Banda della Magliana's former cashier Enrico Nicoletti, who sold insolvent debtors to the clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Si hanno notizie di collaborazione del clan dei Casamonica con l'ex cassiere della Banda della Magliana, Enrico Nicoletti, il quale avrebbe venduto al clan i debitori insolventi al fine di riscuotere i crediti.

descriptions: The two organizations were linked through criminal activities related to the construction of Tor Vergata university.
edges.evidence_quote: la costruzione della seconda università di Roma a Tor Vergata contribuisce a legare il sodalizio criminale tra la Banda della Magliana e il clan Casamonica

descriptions: Members of both organizations were involved together in arms and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 20 aprile 1994 scatta una maxi operazione contro il traffico di armi ... e droga che vede coinvolti esponenti della Banda della Magliana, un agente di Polizia e Dante Casamonica.

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana was linked to Giuseppe Calò, who was associated with the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: sopravvisse a un agguato compiuto da esponenti della Banda della Magliana legati a Giuseppe Calò

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana succeeded the Clan dei marsigliesi after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: La successiva unione di alcune di queste batterie diede poi vita a una vera e propria banda per il controllo totale dei traffici illeciti romani conosciuta come Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: Lubrano-Ligato committed murders on orders of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Abate, collaboratore di giustizia, ed il capoclan Ligato, a cavallo tra gli anni '70 e 90, commisero 477 omicidi, per ordine della Banda della Magliana

descriptions: De Stefano's leader Giorgio had contacts with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: From his law office in Rome, he also had contacts with the Banda della Magliana.

descriptions: Massimo Carminati, a founder of Mafia Capitale, was a former member of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Massimo Carminati, a former member of Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari and Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Mafia Capitale is a successor/continuation of Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: The latest creation of Italian organized crime, Mafia Capitale (which was partially a successor or continuation of Banda della Magliana, involving many former Banda della Magliana members and associates), was mostly disbanded by the police in 2014.

descriptions: Mafia Capitale was founded from the remains of the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: It was founded in the early 2000s by Massimo Carminati from the remains of the Banda della Magliana.

descriptions: The text states they developed in the same years and criminal contexts, but no direct relationship is asserted.
edges.evidence_quote: Sviluppatasi negli stessi anni e negli stessi contesti criminali da cui nacquero a Roma la Banda della Magliana e a Milano la Banda della Comasina

descriptions: NCO established strong ties with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: The NCO also established strong ties with the ... Roman Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Banda della Magliana is listed as an ally of NCO in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuova Grande Camorra Pugliese , Banda della Magliana , Turatello Crew , 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: NCO was allied with Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Banda della Magliana

descriptions: NCO had a representative in the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Cutolo ... indicò Nicolino Selis - uno dei capi della Banda della Magliana ... quale referente della NCO nella capitale

descriptions: Cutolo made agreements with the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: Cutolo stringe anche accordi con le bande lombarde di Renato Vallanzasca ... e la Banda della Magliana romana.

descriptions: NCO had a representative within the Banda della Magliana, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Nicolino Selis ... referente di Cutolo nella Banda della Magliana a Roma.

descriptions: The Corleonesi faction of Cosa Nostra blocked the Banda della Magliana's initiative regarding Moro's release.
edges.evidence_quote: l'iniziativa della banda venne quindi bloccata dalla fazione dei Corleonesi contraria alla liberazione che, attraverso il suo referente romano Giuseppe Calò, intervenne

descriptions: The East Side Gang and the Westside Mob fought in the Crosstown Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fish Market Murders developed into the major gang conflict between the Eastside and Westside Mobs, which would later be known as the Crosstown Mafia War.

descriptions: LaMare's Westside Mob attempted to assassinate Eastside leaders, escalating the Crosstown War.
edges.evidence_quote: LaMare scheduled a meeting between the two divisions of Detroit at a Vernor Highway fish market, where he planned for three gunman to assassinate Eastside mob bosses.

descriptions: The Westside Mob and Eastside Mob were two factions that coexisted under Catalanotte's leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: Catalanotte formed a strong alliance with Wyandotte area beer baron, Joseph Tocco and Hamtramck Mafia leader Chester La Mare. This faction became known as the Westside Mob. ... Bill Tocco and Joe Zerilli were named as Meli's right-hand men. Meli reorganized the group under the 'Eastside Mob' flag.

descriptions: Al-Hijra is the official Kenyan wing of Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Shabaab has Kenya-based affiliates, with al-Hijra as its official Kenyan wing from 2012

CBL/BFL.description: American street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2009 in the Towne Gardens Housing Complex. Engages in drug trafficking, weapons racketeering, and violent conflicts with rival gangs. Members have been convicted under RICO for narcotics conspiracy and attempted murder.

descriptions: The infobox lists Ronnie Gang as a rival of CBL/BFL.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ronnie Gang

descriptions: Dean O'Banion, future North Side leader, was a member of Little Hellions.
edges.evidence_quote: future North Side leader Dean O'Banion , then a member of the juvenile satellite Little Hellions

Fasel Gang.description: A Swiss criminal group active from 1977 to 1991, composed of Jacques Fasel, Daniel Bloch, and a third accomplice. They claimed to fight for anarchism and social justice, committing a series of robberies including a fatal heist. The group was based in Fribourg and all members were eventually captured and imprisoned.

descriptions: Both were major rebel forces in the post-independence period, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: The immediate post-independence period saw the rise of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) and Karen National Union (KNU) in particular as major rebel forces.

descriptions: KNLA is the armed wing of KNU.
edges.evidence_quote: The initial aim of the Karen National Union (KNU) and its armed wing the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA) was to create independent state for the Karen people.

Milwaukee crime family.description: The Milwaukee crime family was an Italian-American Mafia organization based in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, founded around 1918 by Vito Guardalabene. At its peak, it had over 50 made members and was involved in racketeering, skimming from Las Vegas casinos, and other illegal activities. The family was most influential under boss Frank Balistrieri, who led from the early 1960s until his death in 1993. Afterward, the Chicago Outfit absorbed the family, and it ceased to exist as an independent organization by 2024.

South Brooklyn Boys.description: New York City street gang formed in the 1950s from several Italian-American neighborhood gangs in South Brooklyn. Engaged in assault, drug trafficking, extortion, bookmaking, and murder. Loosely affiliated with the Italian-American Mafia. Active from the 1950s to the 2000s.

County Road Cutters.description: A hooligan firm associated with Everton F.C., based in Liverpool, England. Active primarily in the 1980s and early 1990s, known for using Stanley knives and adopting casual clothing. Involved in high-profile clashes, including the 2005 'Battle of Everton Valley' against Manchester United's Red Army.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Chelsea Headhunters.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C. , the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals , as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters , a firm associated with Chelsea F.C. , and the Millwall Bushwackers , a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Millwall Bushwackers.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C. , the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals , as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters , a firm associated with Chelsea F.C. , and the Millwall Bushwackers , a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: The Cutters clashed with the Red Army in 2005, known as 'The Battle of Everton Valley'.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cutters have been involved in a number of high-profile clashes with other firms, most notably in 2005 with the Red Army , a firm associated with Manchester United.

descriptions: The Cutters had a strong rivalry with Seaburn Casuals.
edges.evidence_quote: claims that the Cutters had strong rivalries with hooligan firms of the two Manchester teams, Manchester United F.C. and Manchester City F.C. , the Sunderland firm Seaburn Casuals , as well as with the Chelsea Headhunters , a firm associated with Chelsea F.C. , and the Millwall Bushwackers , a firm associated with Millwall F.C.

descriptions: ETA and Breton Revolutionary Army teamed up to steal explosives.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1999 ETA militants teamed up with the (now self-dissolved) Breton Revolutionary Army to steal explosives from magazines in Brittany.

White Fence.description: White Fence is a predominantly Mexican American street gang based in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles. It claims origins as early as 1900 but emerged in the 1910s as a sports team associated with La Purissima Church. One of the oldest gangs in Los Angeles, it is known for its long-standing rivalry with El Hoyo Maravilla and its early use of firearms. The gang remains active and has expanded its territory to multiple U.S. cities and Central America.

descriptions: White Fence member killed Armenian Power founder.
edges.evidence_quote: Armen 'Silent' Petrosyan, a founder of Armenian Power, was shot to death on May 22, 2000 by Jose Argueta, a member of the Sureño White Fence gang.
